title: T-006 — POST /api/scanner/login + bcrypt PIN verify (06-a)
prompt:
# T-006 — POST /api/scanner/login

PRD: 06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.1 FR-SC-BE-001, §12.1, §12.3 V-SCAN-LOGIN-002, §15.1
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Land the username + PIN login endpoint that creates a `scanner_sessions` row, returns a JWT token + refresh cookie, and enforces 5/10min lockout.

## Implementation contract
1. Next.js Route Handler `apps/scanner/src/app/api/scanner/login/route.ts`:
   - Body schema (Zod): `{ username: string.min(2), pin: string.regex(/^\d{4,6}$/) }`.
   - Lookup user; bcrypt compare against `users.scanner_pin_hash` (rounds 10).
   - Insert scanner_sessions row (device_mode default 'personal' until SCN-012 sets it).
   - Sign JWT with claims per §12.1 (user_id, org_id, site_id null, line_id null, device_mode, roles[]); 5min sliding expiry.
   - Set HttpOnly SameSite=Strict refresh cookie `scanner_session` (8h personal default).
   - Rate limit: track failures in Redis-style key `scanner:login:fail:<user_id>`; 5 fails / 10min ⇒ 429 + audit row.
   - On success/failure write scanner_audit_log row (scan_type='login').
2. Service `apps/scanner/src/server/services/scanner-auth.ts` exposing `loginScanner({username,pin,ip,userAgent})`.

## Files
- apps/scanner/src/app/api/scanner/login/route.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/src/server/services/scanner-auth.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/__tests__/api/scanner-login.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a valid username + PIN, when POST /api/scanner/login runs, then the response 200 carries session_token + expires_at + user{id,name,role} + allowed_sites[] and a scanner_sessions row was inserted
2. Given an invalid PIN attempted 5 times in 10 minutes for the same username, when POST runs again, then it responds 429 SC_RATE_LIMITED and a scanner_audit_log row with result='error', error_code='SC_RATE_LIMITED' is written
3. Given an unknown username, when POST runs, then the response is 401 SC_UNAUTHORIZED with a generic message and the same response time as a wrong-PIN case (timing-safe via bcrypt dummy hash)

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/scanner/__tests__/api/scanner-login.test.ts asserting all three ACs against an in-memory test DB + bcrypt mock
- Run 'pnpm --filter scanner test api/scanner-login'

## Risk red lines
- Do not log the raw PIN anywhere (including request bodies)
- Do not skip the dummy bcrypt path on unknown user (timing-safe)
- Do not hand out the JWT before the scanner_sessions row commit

pipeline_inputs.description: Username + PIN login endpoint that mints scanner_sessions + JWT + refresh cookie with 5/10min lockout.
pipeline_inputs.details: Route handler validates body, bcrypt-compares against users.scanner_pin_hash, inserts scanner_sessions row, signs JWT (5min sliding) and sets HttpOnly SameSite=Strict refresh cookie. Rate limiter enforces V-SCAN-LOGIN-002 (5 fails / 10min lockout). All success and failure paths emit a scanner_audit_log row. Unknown user path uses a dummy bcrypt compare for timing safety.
out_of_scope: Do not implement logout / refresh here (T-007)
Do not implement PIN setup here (T-008)
Do not implement context endpoints here (T-009)

Given an invalid PIN attempted 5 times in 10 minutes for the same username, when POST runs again, then 429 SC_RATE_LIMITED and a scanner_audit_log row with error_code='SC_RATE_LIMITED' is written
Given an unknown username, when POST runs, then 401 SC_UNAUTHORIZED with a generic message and a timing-safe bcrypt dummy path
test_strategy: RED: write scanner-login.test.ts asserting success, lockout after 5 fails, and timing-safe unknown-user path before implementation
risk_red_lines: Do not log the raw PIN
Do not skip dummy bcrypt path on unknown user
Do not return JWT before scanner_sessions commit
